overview.name: Cmdr Raúl Ibáñez-Ochoa
overview.traits: By-the-Book, Professionally Uncomfortable, Will Follow Orders

background: Homeworld: Dipuska (SR 2513 — Industrial, High Population, Imperial). The kind of world that produces reliable, by-the-book Imperial Navy officers. Joined the Navy at 18, served four terms as line officer, commanded two patrol vessels before IMS Resolute Purpose. Has never disobeyed a direct order. Has filed complaints through proper channels on three occasions — all acknowledged and dismissed. He filed the complaints and executed the orders regardless. In-system at Lakamsal for six days under sealed orders. Does not enjoy waiting. Has concluded the situation is not routine. Has not said this to anyone.
education: Imperial Naval Academy. Line officer career track, command certification.
terms: Navy (Line Officer): Tactics (naval)-1, Pilot (spacecraft)-1, Leadership-1, Gun Combat (energy)-1. Initial posting, destroyer escort. Advanced to rank 1.
Navy (Line Officer): Tactics (naval)-2, Pilot (spacecraft)-2, Leadership-2, Electronics (sensors)-1. Command of small craft operations. Advanced to rank 2.
Navy (Line Officer): Gun Combat (energy)-2, Melee (blade)-1, Persuade-1, Admin-0. First independent command. Filed one complaint through proper channels. Advanced to rank 3.

Navy (Line Officer): Tactics (naval)-3, Vacc Suit-0. Command of IMS Resolute Purpose (Type T Patrol Cruiser, 400 tons, TL13). Filed two more complaints. Advanced to rank 4. Equipment: Laser Pistol, ceremonial cutlass (functional), TL13 Combat Armour.
interactions: Role: IMPERIAL ANTAGONIST 3 — The Military Deadline. Controls IMS Resolute Purpose with 30 Marines. Activated by Marchetti-Solis's signal; his sealed orders then direct him to Kethara basin KR-DELTA.

Hard deadline: Resolute Purpose reaches Kethara basin in 90 minutes (infantry approach) or 55 minutes (G-carrier direct strike) from activation signal. 30 TL13 Marines vs 5 SolSec agents is not a fight.
Sealed orders summary: Secure KR-DELTA, take prisoners, minimum force, await NI debriefing team (72 hours ETA). No comms with 291st Fleet until then. He will follow these precisely.

Exploitable constraint: His orders say minimum force and take prisoners. He will honour a surrender signal on open channels — Imperial military law requires him to. He will not be fooled by a fake one. A genuine surrender buys negotiation time.

Personality: Competent, by-the-book, professionally uncomfortable with need-to-know operations. Will treat prisoners correctly under Imperial military law. Will file his after-action report the moment NI debriefing team arrives — regardless of the diplomatic fallout.
His suspicion: He knows this is not routine. He will say nothing about this until after the operation. He will document everything accurately in the after-action report.

armour.name: TL13 Combat Armour (planetside deployment)
armour.traits: Sealed, tactical HUD, squad comms overlay — worn when deploying to surface

equipment.name: Sealed Orders (KR-DELTA)
equipment.traits: Opens on receipt of Marchetti-Solis authorisation signal. Directs him to KR-DELTA — Kethara basin coordinates he has been looking at for six days.

equipment.name: IMS Resolute Purpose (Type T Patrol Cruiser)
equipment.traits: 400 tons, TL13 upgrade. 16 naval crew + 30 Marines. Ship's Boat + G-Carrier. Currently in standby orbit, Lakamsal system.

contacts.kind: Lt. Anya Voss (Marine Commander, IMS Resolute Purpose)
Lt. Commander Marchetti-Solis (NI Detachment Officer — her signal activates his orders)
291st Fleet Command (reporting chain — communications blackout until NI debriefing team arrives)
